pages.raw_text:
A genuine carnival of music
opened at the Spokane theater
last night for the rest of the week
with Saturday matinee under the
auspices of the Woman's club. A
generous part of the proceeds of
these performances will be given
to the food and fuel fund for the
poor of the city.


The program includes selections
from such favorites as THE b.
hemian Girl" Carmen" and THE
Mikado." There are 300 persons in
the numerous scenes and tableaux.
The posing and effects are beauti-
full and We're the recipients of con-
tinuous applause last evening. The
opera selections were rendered in
unvarying good voice. The ballet Is
something fine.


Tonight's program will include a
Carmen scene with Mrs. j. G.. Cum
ningham as Carmencita. DR.. Titus
will sing Don Jose. Mrs. Pearl Hut
ton Schrader will sing special
solo this evening and the other
features of last nights perform.
ance will be repeated. The cast
has been trained by M. A. Agostini.
